commit.message:
UI refactoring:
- Extract 15 self-contained card components from monolithic page files
- Rewrite all pages as thin shells using PageContainer with Grid layout
- Add loading prop to PageContainer for consistent loading states
- Consolidate AlertsPage + NotificationsPage + NotificationPreferences into
single Alerts & Notifications page at /notifications
- Consolidate UsersPage + RolesPage into single User Management page at /admin
- Move OAuthConfigCard to Notifications page (permission-gated)
- Remove dead routes, files, and unused imports

Documentation:
- Add Docusaurus site with 10 user guide pages and 5 API reference pages
- Cover installation, configuration, sensors, alerts, RBAC, and full API docs
- Replace developer-focused markdown docs with user guide
- Brand with Sensor Hub favicon, MUI blue palette, and custom footer

commit.message:
docs: add tmux minimum version note to supported environments

tmux 3.3a+ is recommended; older versions (e.g., Ubuntu 22.04: 3.2a,
RHEL 9: 3.2a) use a show-option fallback for set-clipboard detection.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: yfix
repository.description: Clean and copy terminal text

commit.message:
fix(output): fall back to show-option for tmux < 3.3a set-clipboard

#{set-clipboard} format variable is only available since tmux 3.3a (Sep 2022).
On older tmux (Ubuntu 22.04: 3.2a, RHEL 9: 3.2a, Debian 11: 3.1c), it returns
an empty string, causing the previous code to incorrectly fall back to "on"
even when the user had set-clipboard off.

Fix: extract parse_display_message() as a pure function returning Option for the
set-clipboard field. When None (empty response), fall back to
`tmux show-option -gv set-clipboard`, which works on all tmux versions.

Adds 5 unit tests for parse_display_message covering: on, off, external,
empty (< 3.3a), and no-pid cases.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: enable official Jaeger MCP extension for trace querying

Add jaeger-config.yaml with Jaeger v2 config enabling the built-in
jaeger_mcp extension on port 16687. Update docker-compose to mount
the config and expose the MCP port. Register the MCP server in
.copilot/mcp.json for Copilot integration.

Available MCP tools: get_services, get_span_names, search_traces,
get_trace_topology, get_trace_errors, get_span_details,
get_critical_path, health.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(lsp): add Folding Range handler

Add FoldingRangeHandler implementing IFoldingRangeHandler for
collapsible code regions in the editor.

Supports folding:
- Brace-delimited blocks (functions, if/else, for, while, switch,
  try/catch, interface, record)
- Consecutive single-line comment blocks (// ...)
- Multiline backtick strings

Correctly skips braces inside string literals and comments.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: utah
repository.description: dotnet tool that transpiles a strongly-typed, typescript-inspired scripting language (.shx) into clean, readable bash (.sh)

commit.message:
feat(lsp): add Signature Help handler

Add SignatureHelpHandler implementing ISignatureHelpHandler for
parameter hints when typing function calls.

Features:
- Triggers on '(' and ',' characters
- Shows parameter info for all 20+ built-in namespaces (reuses
  CompletionHandler data)
- Shows parameter info for user-defined functions
- Tracks active parameter via comma counting, respecting nested
  parens, brackets, and string literals
- Parses optional parameters (name?: type) with documentation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: utah
repository.description: dotnet tool that transpiles a strongly-typed, typescript-inspired scripting language (.shx) into clean, readable bash (.sh)

Add GitHub Pages landing site with Tailwind CSS

- Complete responsive landing page for RIVALS game
- Sections: Hero, Features, Weapons, CTA, Footer
- Uses Tailwind CSS for styling
- Ready to deploy on GitHub Pages

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(lsp): add Rename Symbol handler

Add RenameHandler implementing IRenameHandler and IPrepareRenameHandler
for safe refactoring of user-defined symbols (variables, functions,
interfaces, records, and function parameters).

Features:
- PrepareRename validates the symbol is user-defined and renameable
- Blocks renaming of keywords and built-in namespaces
- Finds all occurrences using word-boundary matching
- Skips occurrences inside string literals and comments
- Skips namespace-prefixed identifiers (e.g., console in console.log)
- Validates new name is a valid identifier

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
revert: restore --chat-template-args to disable thinking at server level

The JSON error responses were caused by orphaned backend processes in a
broken state from repeated failed restarts, NOT by the flag itself.

Now that the gateway lifecycle is fixed (PID file race, orphan cleanup,
port-conflict detection), the flag works correctly on a clean start.

Also remove the /no_think prefix from the chat_repl system message —
it is now redundant since thinking is disabled globally at the server.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: mlx-server
repository.description: Local LLM & embedding server for Apple Silicon — OpenAI/Ollama compatible API  gateway powered by MLX

commit.message:
fix: detect and adopt orphaned gateway on port conflict in cmd_serve

When the gateway PID file was stale/missing but a gateway process was
still listening on the port (e.g. after a crash or reboot), cmd_serve
would:
  1. Start a new Python gateway that immediately crashed (port in use)
  2. Poll the health check — which passed because the OLD gateway answered
  3. Show '✅ running' without a valid PID file
  4. Every subsequent ps/run/shutdown would see 'not running'

Fix: before starting a new gateway, check if the port is already serving.
  - If yes: use lsof to find the owning PID, write it to gateway.pid, and
    return 0 (adopt the orphan instead of trying to replace it).
  - If no: start fresh as before.
  - After health check, verify the PID file was actually written; if not,
    surface a clear error instead of silently succeeding with a stale state.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: mlx-server

commit.message:
fix: write PID file after port bind; backend PID files for clean shutdown

Bug 1 - PID file race in gateway:
  serve() wrote the PID file BEFORE binding the port. If the port was
  already occupied (orphaned old gateway), the new process wrote a dead
  PID and crashed immediately. The health check succeeded because the old
  gateway answered, leaving a stale PID file pointing to a dead process.
  Every subsequent 'ps' or 'run' removed the stale file and said 'not running'.
  Fix: write PID file AFTER ThreadedServer() binds successfully.

Bug 2 - Orphaned backends survive gateway restart:
  When the gateway died without SIGTERM (OS kill, crash), its mlx_lm.server
  children became orphans. A new gateway could not register them and would
  try to start new instances on already-occupied ports.
  Fix: each ModelBackend writes its PID to ~/.mlx/backends/<port>.pid at
  start and removes it on clean stop. cmd_shutdown reads those files and
  kills each PID explicitly, even if the gateway PID file is gone.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(atmosphere): add AIO deployment status check via tmux

- Add 'Checking All-in-One Deployment Status' section with tmux
  capture-pane procedure for non-interactive status checks
- Scope deployment, validation, and tempest sections to AIO only
- Update build step 4 to reference new status check procedure
- Add scope notice clarifying AIO vs general operations sections

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: remove --chat-template-args from backend; use /no_think in system message

--chat-template-args '{"enable_thinking": false}' passed to mlx_lm.server
was corrupting Qwen3's chat template processing, causing the model to
respond with a hardcoded JSON error to every single prompt:
  {"error": "Invalid request. Please provide a valid input or clarify your question."}

The flag is removed entirely from _build_command(). Thinking is now
disabled per-conversation via the /no_think soft-switch in the system
message (chat_repl) or prepended to the user message (cmd_chat --no-think).
Both are supported natively by Qwen3 and are no-ops for other models.

NOTE: running backends started with the old flag must be restarted.
Stop them with: mlx-server stop <model>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: add default system prompt in chat REPL to prevent JSON output mode

Qwen3 with mlx_lm.server's enable_thinking:false chat template arg can
push the model into a structured/JSON response mode when no system
message is provided. The model then responds with JSON to all prompts
requests.

Adding an explicit system message anchors the model in natural language
mode and overrides whatever default mlx_lm injects from the template.

Note: the original 'list index out of range' crash (fixed earlier) was
masking this - the JSON output existed from the very first session.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[ir-ra] add theorem-driven support for round-to-plus-inf (#3896)

## Summary

This PR adds theorem-driven IR-RA support for `ROUND_TO_PLUS_INF`
(`FE_UPWARD`).

The previously merged work already:
- kept round-to-nearest on the current tight symmetric path
- used weak fallback for all non-nearest rounding modes

This PR adds the next smallest sound step:
- a dedicated asymmetric tight enclosure for `ROUND_TO_PLUS_INF`
- while keeping other directed modes on the weak fallback path

## Main idea

For round-to-plus-inf, the exact one-step enclosure is modelled as:

- `ra_lo = r`
- `ra_hi = r + B_dir(r)`

where:
- `B_dir(r) = eps_rel_dir * |r| + eps_abs`
- `eps_rel_dir = 2^-52` for binary64
- `eps_rel_dir = 2^-23` for binary32

So this PR implements the proof-aligned single-step shape:

- `fl_RUP(r) ∈ [r, r + B_dir(r)]`

## Main changes

- add `ROUND_TO_PLUS_INF` detection in `apply_ieee754_semantics`
- keep the nearest path unchanged
- add a new asymmetric tight path for `ROUND_TO_PLUS_INF`
- keep `ROUND_TO_MINUS_INF`, `ROUND_TO_ZERO`, and `ROUND_TO_AWAY` on
weak fallback
- add a new regression for the plus-inf tight path
- update the directed weak-fallback regression to use `FE_DOWNWARD`

## Regression coverage

This PR includes:

- `ra-rounding-nearest-tight-bounds`
  - unchanged baseline nearest-mode tight path

- `ra-rounding-plus-inf-tight-bounds`
  - verifies the dedicated tight path for `FE_UPWARD`
  - checks for:
    - `ra_lo_up`
    - `ra_hi_up`
    - `ite`
    - the expected double directed-mode epsilon numerator
    - `VERIFICATION FAILED`

- `ra-rounding-directed-weak-fallback`
  - updated to use `FE_DOWNWARD`
  - verifies that unsupported directed modes still use weak fallback

## Scope

This PR implements only:
- `ROUND_TO_PLUS_INF`

The following modes remain on weak fallback and are deferred:
- `ROUND_TO_MINUS_INF`
- `ROUND_TO_ZERO`
- `ROUND_TO_AWAY`

---------

docs: add Claude Code install instructions to README

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add `--show-call-sites` printing option (#3893)

## Summary

- Adds `--show-call-sites` flag that prints every function call site
with its arguments as a flat list
- Output format: `caller -> callee(arg1, arg2, ...) [file:line]`
- Nested calls are decomposed into temporaries (e.g.
`return_value$_add$5`), showing how return values flow between calls
- Good for building a dependency graph for frontends that use ESBMC

## Example

```
./build/src/esbmc/esbmc --show-call-sites regression/esbmc/print-function-deps_3/main.c

[PROGRESS] Converting

GOTO program creation time: 0.126s
GOTO program processing time: 0.000s
c:@F@__ESBMC_pthread_start_main_hook -> c:@F@__ESBMC_yield() [/home/yiannis/git/esbmc/src/c2goto/library/pthread_lib.c:138]
c:@F@__ESBMC_pthread_end_main_hook -> c:@F@__ESBMC_yield() [/home/yiannis/git/esbmc/src/c2goto/library/pthread_lib.c:151]
```
